SUMMARY: NESTED | FIELD | CONSTR | METHOD DETAIL: FIELD | CONSTR | METHOD

umm.digiquilt.model.works
Class BlockWorks

java.lang.Object
  umm.digiquilt.model.works.BlockWorks
All Implemented Interfaces:
GridDisplayer
public class BlockWorks
extends Object
implements GridDisplayer

Provides abstracted access to the current block, pushing Undos when necessary. Its job is to hold the current block, and notify subscribers when it is changed, and push those changes to the Undo stack.

Version:
$Revision: 1.1 $
Author:
biatekjt, last changed by $Author: biatekjt $ on $Date: 2009-05-28 19:19:53 $

public BlockWorks()
Create a new BlockWorks, with an empty Block and new UndoRedoStack.

Method Detail

addPropertyChangeListener

public void addPropertyChangeListener(String property,
                                      PropertyChangeListener listener)
Add a PropertyChangeListener to this object. Properties are:
"grid": The current Grid.
"currentBlock": The current Block.

Parameters:
property -
listener -

public void removePropertyChangeListener(String property,
                                         PropertyChangeListener listener)
Remove this PropertyChangeListener.

Parameters:
property -
listener -

setGrid

public void setGrid(Grid grid)
Set the current Grid.

Specified by:
setGrid in interface GridDisplayer
Parameters:
grid -

getGrid

public Grid getGrid()
Returns:
the Grid currently being used

getUndoRedoStack

public UndoRedoStack getUndoRedoStack()
Returns:
this BlockWorks' UndoRedoStack

getCurrentBlockClone

public Block getCurrentBlockClone()
Get a clone of the current block.

Returns:
clone of the current block

setCurrentBlock

public void setCurrentBlock(Block newBlock)
Replace the current block with a new one. Note: This does not push an Undo, since it's used by the Undos themselves and when a file is loaded from XML.

Parameters:
newBlock -

setNewUndoStack

public void setNewUndoStack(UndoRedoStack newStack)
Replace the current UndoRedoStack with a new one. The button listeners from the old one will be transferred to the new one.

Parameters:
newStack -

performUndo

public void performUndo()
Called when a block change is undone. Sets the current block to the given new block and takes care of making the undo redoable.

performRedo

public void performRedo()
Called when a block change is redone. Will set the current block to the given block, and make sure that the redone change is undoable again.

clear

public void clear()
Clear the current block.

See Also:
Block.clear()

getSize

public int getSize()
Get the size of the current block, in patches.

Returns:
size of the block
See Also:
Block.getSize()

getSideSize

public int getSideSize()
Method to get the height/width of this block, in patches

Returns:
height/width of block
See Also:
Block.getSideSize()

getBlockCoverage

public Fraction getBlockCoverage(Fabric fabric)
Get the fraction of the current block covered with this fabric.

Parameters:
fabric - the desired fabric
Returns:
what fraction is covered by that fabric
See Also:
Block.getBlockCoverage(Fabric)

replaceFabricInBlock

public void replaceFabricInBlock(Fabric replaceThis,
                                 Fabric withThis)
Replace one fabric with another in the current block.

Parameters:
replaceThis - Replaces this fabric...
withThis - with this one.
See Also:
Block.replaceFabricInBlock(Fabric, Fabric)

swapFabricInBlock

public void swapFabricInBlock(Fabric one,
                              Fabric two)
Swap two fabrics in the current block.

Parameters:
one - the first fabric to be swapped
two - the second fabric to be swapped
See Also:
Block.swapFabricInBlock(Fabric, Fabric)

replacePatchInBlock

public void replacePatchInBlock(Patch replaceThis,
                                Patch withThis)
Replace one patch with another in the current block.

Parameters:
replaceThis - Replaces this patch...
withThis - with this one.
See Also:
Block.replacePatchInBlock(Patch, Patch)

swapPatchesInBlock

public void swapPatchesInBlock(Patch swapThis,
                               Patch andThis)
Swap two patches in the current block.

Parameters:
swapThis - Swap this patch...
andThis - and this one.
See Also:
Block.swapPatchesInBlock(Patch, Patch)

setPatch

public void setPatch(Patch aPatch,
                     int location)
Set a patch to a certain position on the current block.

Parameters:
aPatch - The input patch
location - The position to place this patch
See Also:
Block.setPatch(Patch, int)

setPatch

public void setPatch(Patch aPatch,
                     int location,
                     boolean undo)
Set a patch to a certain position on the current block, with the option of not pushing an undo. Use with caution! This is provided so that when a patch is moved from the current block to the current block, the inbetween state of the block can be discarded.

Parameters:
aPatch - The input patch
location - The position to place this patch
undo - Push an undo onto the stack?
See Also:
Block.setPatch(Patch, int)

getPatch

public Patch getPatch(int location)
Returns the patch at a specific location in the current block.

Parameters:
location - The location of the patch
Returns:
the patch
See Also:
Block.getPatch(int)
